metadata.title: Claude, Unstuck
metadata.subtitle: Troubleshooting and Staying Current
metadata.audience: Practitioners and makers following the channel's Claude workflows
metadata.one_idea: When something breaks or doesn't match the screen, don't chase the surface — return to the durable concept (what the plugin is for, what it can and can't do) and check the official docs for the current specifics; the interface dates, the mental model doesn't.

props.command: My plugin isn't working — and half the fixes I find online don't match what I'm seeing on my screen. Where do I even start?
output: Start from the concept, not the screen —
then check the official docs for what's current.

beats.narration_text: Salaam — this is Kore, in for Humanitarians AI. A plugin misbehaves. A connection fails. Or the guide you're reading doesn't match your screen. The move isn't to chase the surface — it's to return to what the plugin is for, and check the official docs for what's true today.

props.command: My plugin isn't working — and half the fixes I find online don't match what I'm seeing on my screen. Where do I even start?
output: Start from the concept, not the screen —
then check the official docs for what's current.

beats.narration_text: First, the mindset.

Things will go wrong. Plugins won't behave as expected, connections drop, results disappoint. The real danger isn't the bug — it's that quiet frustration hardens into abandonment. Diagnosing is how you stay in the game.
beats.show: A field of small failure marks; one of them hardens and darkens into an X labeled 'abandonment'.

props.label: Frustration → abandonment.
props.caption: Things will go wrong. Plugins won't behave as expected, connections drop, results disappoint. The real danger isn't the

beats.narration_text: So here's the discipline. The interface is the surface — buttons, menus, wording — and it changes constantly. Underneath sits the durable layer: what the plugin is for, what it can and can't do. You troubleshoot from there.
beats.show: A shifting surface layer slides and re-labels itself while a solid base line holds perfectly steady beneath it.

beats.narration_text: Most breakage takes one of five shapes.

props.title: The Common Failures
props.sub: Most breakage takes one of five shapes.

beats.narration_text: Five failures recur. The plugin won't appear. A service won't authenticate. Results come back generic. Everything runs slow. Or a slash-command isn't recognized. Each one has a durable cause — and a durable fix.
beats.show: Five caution chips resolve onto the stage in turn.

beats.narration_text: Plugin missing after you installed it? Plugins load when Cowork starts — so a mid-session install may not show. Close Cowork completely, reopen it, and it loads. Still nothing? Reinstall it from the plugin's sidebar.
beats.show: A window closes fully and reopens; on reload, the new plugin tile pops into the active list.

props.label: Reopen to reload.
props.caption: Plugin missing after you installed it? Plugins load when Cowork starts — so a mid-session install may not show. Close Co

beats.narration_text: A service that won't authenticate almost always means stale credentials — tokens expire, passwords change, access gets revoked. Disconnect the service and reconnect it. And if your company gates third-party access, you may need IT's approval.
beats.show: A still of an expired key / rejected sign-in; slow push-in.
beats.pantry_note: Tier 1 illustrative: an old key or a lock tagged 'expired', documentary duotone. No real logos or product UI.

beats.narration_text: Plugin works, but the results feel generic? That's not a fault — the defaults are deliberately broad. Feed it your context — brand voice, your process, your standards — and the output sharpens past the point where it's actually useful.
beats.show: A 'specificity' meter sits mid-scale on 'default', then jumps past a threshold to 'your context'.

beats.narration_text: Slow operations usually reach outside your machine — a CRM query, a database lookup, web research all cost more than local work. The fix is scope. Ask for one date range, one folder — not everything, everywhere at once.
beats.show: A huge query set shrinks to a single slice; the latency bar falls right along with it.

beats.narration_text: A slash-command not recognized? Make sure the plugin is installed and enabled — then type a single slash to see every command available right now. If it's still missing, the plugin most likely needs a restart.
beats.show: Onda code-block: the composer types '/' and a command list resolves; then '/plugins'.

beats.narration_text: When you're stuck, run the loop.

props.title: The Four Steps
props.sub: When you're stuck, run the loop.

beats.narration_text: Two quick diagnostics first. Type slash-plugins to see what's installed and active — your first check for anything plugin-related. And if one plugin's misbehaving, disable it, then re-enable it; that reloads its config and clears most transient glitches.
beats.show: A /plugins panel lists active plugins; one toggles off, then on, and settles.

props.label: Check, then toggle.
props.caption: Two quick diagnostics first. Type slash-plugins to see what's installed and active — your first check for anything plugi

beats.narration_text: Then the loop that resolves most problems, in order. One: is the plugin installed and active? Two: are its connections authorized? Three: try a simpler request to isolate the issue. Four: restart Cowork. Most trouble dies inside those four steps.
beats.show: Four steps light in sequence; a 'resolved' counter climbs toward full as each one clears.

beats.narration_text: That third step carries more than its size. Shrink the request until it works — then grow it back. The exact moment it breaks tells you precisely where the problem lives.
beats.show: A still of one thread lifted clear of a tangled knot — isolation. Slow push toward the freed strand.
beats.pantry_note: Tier 1 illustrative: a single thread pulled clear of a knot, documentary duotone. No product UI.

beats.narration_text: Now the part this whole chapter is really about.

props.title: Staying Current
props.sub: Now the part this whole chapter is really about.

beats.narration_text: Plugins don't sit still. Anthropic improves the official ones; community creators ship their own updates. Each release can add capabilities, fix bugs, or run faster — which means the thing you learned last month may behave differently today.
beats.show: A still of layered, versioned plates stacking upward — evolution over time. Slow drift up the stack.
beats.pantry_note: Tier 1 illustrative: stacked versioned cards or growth rings; no legible version numbers, no product UI.

beats.narration_text: And that's the catch. Cowork handles most updates automatically — so the interface shifts underneath you. Anything written down about the screen starts dating the moment it's written. The concept underneath does not.
beats.show: A timeline where interface labels churn and re-version tick by tick, while a single concept line runs flat and unbroken beneath them.

beats.narration_text: So verify specifics against sources that stay current. Each plugin's own docs — on its GitHub page and inside Cowork. The plugin directory, where others have already solved your problem. And the official channels — the Anthropic blog and Claude's release notes — for what actually changed.
beats.show: SourceFlow: three living sources flow in on the left and resolve into 'what's true today' on the right.

beats.narration_text: Make it a habit. Set a monthly reminder to browse the directory and see what's new. And when a plugin you rely on changes, read the changelog — a new feature might be exactly the thing you were missing.
beats.show: A still of a calendar with a monthly mark, changelog pages fluttering above it. The camera begins a slow descent.
beats.pantry_note: Tier 1 illustrative: a calendar plus loose changelog pages, documentary duotone. Framed to allow a downward move into B15's base. Continuity into B16 (vox_run R1).

beats.narration_text: Because that's the real skill here. Not memorizing today's menus — but knowing what the plugin is for, and where to check what's current. The interface dates. The mental model doesn't.
beats.show: The continuous descent lands off the fluttering pages onto a solid foundation block — the durable layer from B02 returns. A terracotta underline draws under 'the mental model'.
beats.pantry_note: Tier 1 illustrative: same scene as B15, camera settles on a solid stone foundation beneath the pages. Continuity with B15 (vox_run R1).

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. When a plugin breaks, don't chase the surface — return to what it's for. Most failures have durable causes: not active, stale credentials, un-customized defaults, external scope, or a needed restart. Four steps clear most of them — active, authorized, simpler, restart. And the interface will keep changing, so verify the specifics in the official docs. The screen dates; the mental model doesn't.

props.command: A plugin I installed isn't showing up in Cowork and I'm not sure why. Diagnose it with me: ask whether I restarted Cowork after installing, whether /plugins lists it as active, and whether its connections are authorized. Then give me the four-step check to run in order, and point me to the one official source to check in case something changed.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this into Claude: a plugin I installed isn't showing up, and I'm not sure why — diagnose it with me. Ask whether I restarted after installing, whether slash-plugins lists it as active, and whether its connections are authorized. Then give me the four-step check in order, and point me to the one official source to check in case something changed. It turns this chapter into a troubleshooter that walks your actual problem — run it the next time something breaks.
